game_identity.visual_style: Cartoonish 2D vector art with physics-driven rigid bodies

core_loop.one_sentence: Players take turns aiming and firing various weapons at an enemy castle to destroy its structure and deplete its health before their own castle is destroyed.
core_loop.player_goal: Reduce the enemy castle's health to 0% by hitting it with projectiles.
core_loop.failure_or_pressure: The enemy castle fires back every turn, steadily destroying the player's castle and reducing its health.
core_loop.why_it_is_fun: Satisfying physics-based destruction, visual feedback of crumbling castles, and the tactical choice of different projectile types.

player_controls.target: A specific character inside the player's castle
player_controls.result: Displays a dotted trajectory line indicating the path of the projectile
Fires the character's specific weapon along the aimed trajectory

visible_ui.purpose: Indicates the remaining health/structural integrity of each castle
Emphasizes the competitive, head-to-head nature of the game
Helps the player aim their shot before firing
Displays the game title 'Castle Clashers' and a 'PLAY' call-to-action button

timeline_beats.gameplay_meaning: Establishes the threat and the turn-based nature of the game.
timeline_beats.playable_relevance: Good hook to show the player they are under attack and need to retaliate.

timeline_beats.gameplay_meaning: Introduces the core aiming mechanic and single-projectile weapon.
timeline_beats.playable_relevance: The primary interaction the user will perform in the playable ad.

timeline_beats.gameplay_meaning: Demonstrates weapon variety (multi-rocket attack) and significant physics destruction.
timeline_beats.playable_relevance: Shows progression and more satisfying visual feedback to keep the user engaged.
Highlights character variety, though might be cut for a shorter playable ad.
The final destination of the ad, prompting the app store click.

mechanics.name: Trajectory Aiming
mechanics.description: Dragging in the opposite direction of the desired shot to draw a predictive arc.

mechanics.name: Physics-Based Destruction
mechanics.description: Castles are made of individual blocks/pieces that break, turn black, and fall based on projectile impact.

mechanics.name: Turn-Based Combat
mechanics.description: The player and the AI take alternating turns firing at each other.

mechanics.name: Distinct Unit Attacks
mechanics.description: Different characters fire different projectiles (e.g., single rocket, spread rockets).

feedback_and_juice.visual_or_audio_feedback: Explosions, smoke particles, screen shake, and castle debris flying outward.
Health percentage numbers update dynamically, and damaged sections turn black/charred.
A clear, dotted white line appears to guide the player's shot.
A 'RIP' tombstone appears where the character was standing.

must_include: One player character and one enemy castle
can_simplify: Reduce the number of player characters to just one or two to avoid choice paralysis
Script the enemy's turns to be quick and predictable
Simplify the physics engine to ensure smooth performance on mobile web
should_avoid: Complex menus or character selection screens before gameplay
Long waiting times during the enemy's turn

variation_hooks.effect: Changes the visual explosion and the pattern of destruction (e.g., single massive hit vs. widespread scatter damage).
Alters the difficulty and the visual satisfaction of the collapse.
variation_hooks.safe_range_or_values: Tall tower, wide fortress, two separate pillars

variation_hooks.effect: Changes how accurately the enemy hits the player's castle.
variation_hooks.safe_range_or_values: Always misses slightly, hits center mass, targets specific characters

open_questions_or_assumptions: Assumption: The player can choose which character to fire by tapping/dragging on them, rather than the game forcing a specific order.
Question: Does the health percentage calculate based on the number of remaining blocks, or is it a hidden HP pool for the overall structure?
Question: Is there a wind mechanic affecting the trajectory that isn't explicitly shown in the UI?
